figures.caption: Table 2: Performance comparison of various MLLM backbones with and without 3D-MIX on GR00T-style VLA architecture across SIMPLER and LIBERO benchmarks.

Figure 1: Overview of nine VGGT fusion schemes evaluated in our pilot study. Each scheme integrates VGGTderived 3D geometric features into the VLA pipeline at a different location or via a different mechanism, ranging from early token-level injection to training-time alignment and cross-attention fusion.
Table 3: Performance comparison of various MLLM backbones with and without 3D-MIX on π-style VLA architecture across SIMPLER and LIBERO benchmarks.
Figure 3: Ablation study results. Each subfigure corresponds to one ablation experiment: (a) VGGT Frozen vs. Trainable. (b) 3D Information Sensitivity. (c) Sparse Layer Fusion Depth (π-style).
Table 1: Comparison of nine VGGT fusion schemes on the SIMPLER (out-of-domain, real-to-sim) and LIBERO (in-domain, multi-task) benchmarks.
